item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The skip is pretty good. I like the imprecise trigger for its effect, makes it feel like something GAW would create.
Also, 4.2.0.69. +1 just for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Back in February of 2017 apparently I didn't know what made a good SCP. This is awesome.

Happily upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Assuming that the forum in question is MTG Salvation, I think the "shit" at least should be changed to a "" (and possibly the fukken, I'm unsure as to the details of how MTGSalvation's autofilter works).

Just for realism's sake. Big fan of the rest of this, especially as someone who resents "collectors" who extol the virtues of the Reserved List.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Weeeell this is a GAW SCP sooo, irrelevant much?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think GAW just likes making fun of Dr. Wondertainment, they did make the Misters Against Weed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm…. The wondertainment referrence feels somewhat shoehorned to me. Other than that it's a very fresh and creative idea and as a former collector of card games, this one speaks to me and brings back good memories (Chaos Confetti anyone? ;) )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This game was lit! I played it in 8th grade (So like 2 years ago) I did waste a lot of money on it though…
Edit* This is like those fake cards like the spin-off of Black Lotus, Blacker Lotus.
This made me laugh as well as get it as a story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the story on this, and if this were a -J, I'd upvote for that fucking IP address because lol.

But as it is, hmm, I dunno. Maybe it's lingering knee-jerk resistance to GAW. Or the fact that I never could get into Magic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who followed the progress of development of CCGs over the decades (and has a friend who works for WotC), this does amuse me. If you understood how the early days of the game were really a race for the rare cards that would let you beat anyone without them, this is particularly fascinating.

…Although I had been under the opinion that the true CCG economic frenzy had burned itself out a recession or two ago.

(Still, Mox Gems and the Black Lotus were where it was at in the early days, preferably with a Fireball and Channel to win on the first turn before your opponent could even start.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was halfway through when I thought, "This sounds like something GAW would do." Ha-HAH!

My personal exposure to MtG (or as I call it Tragic this Gathering) was through my older son. He was enthusiastic about trying to teach me, but it felt like doing algebra word problems in my head. The artwork was enjoyable, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am torn on this. While I do love MtG (most of my writing experience before coming here was MtG fanfic), I feel like this is a bit too on the nose and fourth wall-breaking for my taste. And still it is really funny, and quite well written. I will have to come back to this. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great… an overpriced card game. Then again, I did get my valentine a Yu-Gi-Oh deck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, this is pretty well done; it's charming, funny, and original. +1/+1.
I usually don't like straight up pop culture refferences, but holy shit this works well, real well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, i dunno.

As someone who lived off of Magic1 when it came out, i was hooked early and effectivly, which is cool. But the story was meh in my opinion, but the anomaly was pretty cool and original.

No vote.

Footnotes
1. Totally not exaggerating.

item.title: SCP-2658
item.content:encoded:
Hey, it's my first SCP! Big thanks to Scantron, kinchtheknifeblade, Rachel, and probably some other people I'm forgetting for their help!

I first had the idea for this after an incredibly poorly thought out reply to controversy on Reddit a year or so ago, but it's taken me a while to actually hash it out. I know 4.2.0.69 currently points to the middle of a reservoir, but hopefully it'll get used by November of 2018.

I made the images myself.
